html:
Filip or Philip is a male name of Greek origin, derived from Philippos, meaning 'friend of horses'. During the Middle Ages, the form Filpus was used. The female equivalent is Filippa.

In Sweden, the name has been very popular as a given name since the 1980s. Between 1998 and 2001, Filip fluctuated between first and second place on the popularity charts. As of December 31, 2013, there were a total of 40,814 men in Sweden named Filip or Philip, of whom 28,558 used it as their preferred name. In 2011, 614 boys were given the name, with 423 using it as their nickname. Approximately two-thirds (17,906) of those who use Filip as a nickname spell it with an "F".

Name day: In Sweden, May 2nd. In the Swedish-speaking Finnish calendar of names, Filip's name day is March 8th, since the introduction of a separate calendar for Swedish speakers in Finland in 1929.

== People named Filip/Philip ==

=== Royal figures ===

==== Sweden ==== Filip, king (died 1118) Filip, prince (died around 1200), son of King Erik the Holy Filip, prince (died 1251), son of King Knut II Karl Filip, prince (1601-1622), son of King Karl IX Carl Philip, prince (born 1979), son of King Carl XVI Gustaf

==== Belgium ==== Philippe of Belgium

==== Denmark ==== Filip Ferdinand, prince (1519-1520), son of King Christian II

==== France ==== Philip I Philip II Philip III Philip IV (Philip I of Navarre) Philip V (Philip II of Navarre) Philip VI

==== Macedonia ==== Philip I Philip II, father of Alexander the Great Philip III Philip IV Philip V

==== Navarre ==== Philip III

==== Seleucid Empire ==== Philip I Philadelphos Philip II Filoromeo

==== Spain ==== Philip I (Castile) Philip II (Philip I of Portugal) Philip III (Philip II of Portugal) Philip IV (Philip III of Portugal) Philip V Felipe VI

==== United Kingdom ==== Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh

==== German principalities ==== Philip I, ruling Landgrave of Hesse, known as the Noble Philip of Swabia

=== Other people with the first name Filip ===

  • Filippus, antipope
  • Carl Philipp Emanuel Bach, German composer
  • Filip Ericsson, Swedish sailor, Olympic gold medalist in 1912
  • Filip Hammar, Swedish author, journalist, and television host
  • Filip Helander, Swedish football player
  • Philip Hone, American politician
  • Filip Johansson, football and bandy player
  • Philip Kirkorov, Bulgarian-Armenian singer, actor, musical artist, and television personality
  • Filip Knutsson (13th century), son of Knut Långe, executed by Birger jarl in 1251.
  • Filip Knutsson (Aspenäsätten), died before November 28, 1335.
  • Filip Kristensson, politician (Fp), county governor
  • Phil Lynott, Irish musician and leader of the hard rock group Thin Lizzy
  • Philippe Pétain, French marshal
  • Philippe Petit, French tightrope walker
  • Philip Roth, American author
  • Philip Sandblom, professor of surgery, university rector
  • Philipp Scheidemann, German politician, chancellor
  • Philip von Schantz, artist, painter, and graphic designer
  • Filip Skoglund, bandy player
  • Georg Philipp Telemann, German composer
  • Philip Zandén, Swedish actor and director

=== Fictional characters with the first name Filip ===

  • Filip, rock bouncer in Ragnar Josephsons play "Kanske en diktare" from 1932.

== Different spellings == Below is a list of different spellings of the name Filip. The number in parentheses indicates the number of people in Sweden with that spelling (December 31, 2013).

  • Filip (25229)
  • Philip (15629)
  • Phillip (293)
  • Filiph (274)
  • Philipp (197)
  • Fhilip (78)
  • Filipp (33)
  • Philiph (18)
  • Fillip (15)
  • Phillipp (2)
  • Fiilip (1)
  • Fillipp (1)

== Sources ==

How certain are we about this?

For people from the Middle Ages, there are often contemporary written sources (letters, chronicles, church documents), so the main points are usually well-documented. However, these sources were mostly written by the Church and those in power, and they are unevenly preserved, which colors and limits the picture.
tldr: The text describes the name "Filip," its history, how many people in Sweden have this name, and which famous individuals bear it.
lattlast:
The name Filip originates from the Greek word for "horse friend." It has been a very popular name in Sweden since the 1980s and exists in many different spellings, with 'Filip' being the most common form.

Throughout history, there have been many famous people named Filip. These include kings from countries like Spain and France, as well as renowned athletes, musicians, and authors from both Sweden and other nations.

In Sweden, the name day is celebrated on May 2nd, while in Finland it is celebrated on March 8th. The text also indicates that many different spellings of the name exist, but that most Swedes write it with an "F."
